L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the JSON parsing logic. When processing incoming JSON data, the deserializer honors a special type annotation field that allows an attacker to specify arbitrary modules and classes to be dynamically imported and instantiated with attacker-controlled arguments. This can be exploited to invoke dangerous system-level classes, leading to arbitrary command execution.

L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the JSON parsing logic. When processing incoming JSON data, the deserializer honors a special type annotation field that allows an attacker to specify arbitrary modules and classes to be dynamically imported and instantiated with attacker-controlled arguments. This can be exploited to invoke dangerous system-level classes, leading to arbitrary command execution.

ground_truth.reason: The custom JSON deserializer uses a __type__ field to dynamically import and instantiate arbitrary classes with attacker-controlled arguments, enabling remote code execution via classes such as subprocess.Popen.
